scenario: Aurora Marketplace plans a Camunda 8.8 integration that handles 6000 requests per day from a customer-facing single page app. Engineers want to import the Camunda 8 TypeScript SDK directly into the browser bundle, but they must first confirm the runtime the SDK actually supports before committing to the architecture.
question: Which runtime does the Camunda 8.8 TypeScript SDK require, and how should browser applications be served?

options.text: Deno is the supported runtime for the SDK and a separate browser bundle is shipped from the same package for single page apps in 8.8
Node.js is the supported runtime for the SDK and browser applications must use the @camunda8/orchestration-cluster-api package instead
Bun is the supported runtime for the SDK and a small shim allows the same package to also run inside an evergreen web browser tab
Any modern JavaScript runtime including web browsers can host the SDK because it ships as an isomorphic build starting in 8.8

a.text: Incorrect. The prerequisites table names Node.js as the SDK runtime, not Deno, and no browser bundle is shipped from the same package; browsers must use a different package.

b.text: Correct. The prerequisites state the SDK runs in Node.js and cannot run in a web browser due to technical limitations; browser apps must use @camunda8/orchestration-cluster-api.

c.text: Incorrect. The SDK targets Node.js according to the prerequisites; Bun is not listed and no in-browser shim is provided for the camunda-8-js-sdk package.

d.text: Incorrect. The SDK explicitly cannot run in a web browser due to technical limitations; browser applications must adopt @camunda8/orchestration-cluster-api.

explanation: The TypeScript SDK prerequisites require Node.js and call out that the package cannot run in a web browser; browser applications use the @camunda8/orchestration-cluster-api client instead.
